no_gaps_directive.primitive: Reuse the existing safe-Rust cache-friendly matmul_2d kernel for production Dot/DotGeneral rank-2 F64 matmul.
alien_graveyard_lineage: /data/projects/alien_cs_graveyard/alien_cs_graveyard.md references dense linear algebra, cache locality, tiled/SIMD kernels, and communication-avoiding linear algebra as high-leverage numeric-kernel primitives.

profile_backed_target.bead_source: frankenjax-mcqr.23 was ready and labelled [perf][no-gaps]. It cites mcqr.22's profile-proven matmul_2d kernel and the production Dot/DotGeneral caller gap.
profile_backed_target.baseline_command: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/frankenjax-lax-dot-baseline-pass34-20260603 rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- eval/dot_256x256x256_f64 --sample-size 30 --measurement-time 3 --warm-up-time 1
profile_backed_target.note: A production-path Criterion bench for eval_primitive(Primitive::Dot) on 256x256 @ 256x256 F64 matrices was added before the production source lever so the baseline measured the old generic Dot path.

optimization.lever: Route plain rank-2 F64 matrix multiplication in eval_dot and the equivalent no-batch DotGeneral signature through matmul_2d.
optimization.guard: lhs.dtype == F64; rhs.dtype == F64; lhs.rank == 2; rhs.rank == 2; Dot uses lhs last dim and rhs penultimate dim; DotGeneral additionally requires lhs_contracting=[1], rhs_contracting=[0], no batch dims, lhs_free=[0], rhs_free=[1].

after.command: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/frankenjax-lax-dot-after-pass34-final-20260603 rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- eval/dot_256x256x256_f64 --sample-size 30 --measurement-time 3 --warm-up-time 1

benchmark.worker_caveat: Baseline and final-source after measurements were both remote RCH measurements but on different workers. The observed 68.23x speedup is large enough that cross-worker variance does not affect the keep decision.

isomorphism_proof.ordering_preserved: Yes. Outputs remain row-major: for each lhs row i, rhs column j is emitted in ascending j order before the next i.
isomorphism_proof.floating_point: Identical for the guarded path. matmul_2d uses i-k-j loop order, but every output cell c[i,j] receives contributions for k=0..K-1 in the same ascending order as the old i-j-k dot_accumulate loop.
isomorphism_proof.dtype_and_shape: Yes. The guard only accepts declared F64 x F64 rank-2 tensors, emits DType::F64, and passes the same output_dims through dot_output_value.
isomorphism_proof.malformed_tensor_behavior: Preserved. The fast path extracts each literal via as_f64 and returns the same TypeMismatch detail for non-numeric lhs/rhs elements; non-F64/rank/batch/signature cases fall through to the generic path.
isomorphism_proof.dot_general_scope: Only the standard rank-2 matmul signature lhs_contracting=[1], rhs_contracting=[0], no batch dims is routed. Batched, transposed, outer, vector, higher-rank, and non-F64 DotGeneral paths are unchanged.
isomorphism_proof.tie_breaking: N/A. Dot has no comparisons or ties.
isomorphism_proof.rng: N/A. Dot is deterministic and consumes no random state.
